dict.string: $(DEVELOPMENT_LANGUAGE)
PsyClinicAI
$(EXECUTABLE_NAME)
$(PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER)
psyclinicai
APPL
$(FLUTTER_BUILD_NAME)
$(FLUTTER_BUILD_NUMBER)
LaunchScreen
Main
PsyClinicAI uses the camera so you can attach intake-form photos or scan insurance cards into the chart. Nothing is uploaded without your tap-to-send confirmation.
PsyClinicAI uses the microphone for live session transcription. Audio is processed on this device — only the redacted transcript text leaves the device.
PsyClinicAI uses on-device Speech Recognition so session audio never has to be uploaded to a server for transcription.
PsyClinicAI may read images you choose to attach to a clinical note. The app never reads photos in the background.
PsyClinicAI writes the DSAR data-export ZIP (GDPR Article 20) to your Photos so you can keep a personal copy. No PHI is sent to iCloud without your action.
PsyClinicAI uses Face ID to unlock your local chart after auto-logout. The biometric template stays in the device Secure Enclave and never reaches our servers.
PsyClinicAI reads only the PHQ-9 and GAD-7 mood-screening scores you opt in to share, so clinicians can see trends alongside the session note.
PsyClinicAI writes PHQ-9 and GAD-7 results back to the Health app when you complete a screener, so the score history travels with you.
PsyClinicAI uses the local network only during a telehealth session to set up the encrypted WebRTC video call. We never scan the local network outside of an active session.
PsyClinicAI optionally reads your contacts so you can pick an emergency-contact phone number for a patient intake. The intake form is the only place this access is used.
